steps.title: Isolate the 13x term
steps.content:
Start with $13x + 50 = 180$. Subtract $50$ from both sides:
$$13x = 180 - 50 = 130$$
Any equation equivalent to $13x = 130$ has the same solution as the original.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $13x = 50$ — gives $x = \frac{50}{13}$, not $\frac{130}{13} = 10$. Different solution. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $13x = 80$ — gives $x = \frac{80}{13}$. Different solution. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: $13x = 130$ — gives $x = 10$, the same solution as the original equation. Correct.
  • Choice D: $13x = 180$ — gives $x = \frac{180}{13}$. Different solution. Incorrect.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
